subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (Step 0)
- **Primary mode structure** — Exactly **2 teams** compete.
- **Team sizing rule** — Teams should be **roughly the same size**.
- **Go-first scoring offset** — First team starts at **0 points**, second team starts at **1 point**.
- **Cooperative mode note** — Rulebook says co-op mode works very well with **2-5 players**, but can be played at **any size**.
- **No per-count component swap** — The excerpt shows no component count that changes by player count.

#### Core Device Components
- **Main device** — 1 Wavelength device (wheel + hidden target + blue screen + red dial).
- **Card deck** — 1 deck of double-sided Wavelength cards.
- **Team score tokens** — 2 head tokens (one per team).
- **Opponent guess token** — 1 LEFT/RIGHT guessing token.

#### Setup-Visible Counts
- **Tokens inserted at setup** — 3 total (2 scoring heads + 1 guessing piece).
- **Card draw cadence** — 1 card drawn per round in competitive mode.

#### Card-Count Data Caveat
- **Rulebook excerpt limit** — Provided text does not show a numeric total for cards.
- **Practical use** — Use the full included card deck for your edition.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Board Layout Sequence
1. **Seat the device** — Place the Wavelength device into the tray slot.
2. **Set score tokens** — Insert both team head tokens into score tracks.
3. **Set guess token** — Insert LEFT/RIGHT guess token in its slot.
4. **Place card deck** — Keep Wavelength cards as draw pile near device.
5. **Check privacy angle** — Ensure active Psychic can open/close screen privately.

#### Visibility Rules
- **Public** — Spectrum card text and final dial position.
- **Private** — Hidden target location during Psychic setup.
- **Rotation flow** — Turn the box toward active team after clue is given.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Team Setup
- **Team split** — Divide players into 2 teams of roughly equal size.
- **First Psychics** — Choose one Psychic (clue giver) on each team.
- **Default names** — Teams may use Left Brain and Right Brain, or custom names.

#### Starting Score Setup
- **First team score** — 0 points.
- **Second team score** — 1 point.

#### Communication Restriction
- **Psychic silence lock** — After giving clue, Psychic may not clarify verbally or nonverbally.
- **Face rule** — Psychic keeps neutral expression after clue.

#### Player-Count Sections
--- 2-5 Players ---
- **Co-op suitability** — Rulebook notes cooperative mode works especially well at this size.

--- Any Size ---
- **Co-op allowance** — Cooperative mode can still be played.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### First Round Start Sequence
1. **Choose first team** — Decide which team acts first.
2. **Close screen** — Psychic closes blue screen completely.
3. **Draw card** — Psychic draws 1 Wavelength card and chooses one side.
4. **Read spectrum** — Psychic reads the two opposite concepts aloud.
5. **Randomize target** — Spin wheel with screen closed.
6. **Inspect target** — Open screen fully and view target center.
7. **Give clue** — Speak one clue on the spectrum.
8. **Re-close screen** — Close screen and rotate box to Psychic’s team.
9. **Start Team Phase** — Teammates discuss and set dial.

#### Legality Notes
- **Full-open requirement** — Psychic should open screen fully when viewing target.
- **Invalid target reveal** — If center wedge is completely not visible, re-spin.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Phase Order
1. **Psychic Phase** — Active Psychic prepares and gives one clue.
2. **Team Phase** — Psychic team discusses and sets dial.
3. **Left/Right Phase** — Other team guesses LEFT or RIGHT of dial.
4. **Scoring Phase** — Reveal target and award points.

#### Turn Passing Rule
- **Default pass** — After scoring, the other team becomes active.
- **Exception** — Catch-up rule may grant immediate extra turn to same team.

#### Player-Count Structure
- **Core competitive mode** — Always two teams, regardless of total player count.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Psychic Actions
- **Card draw** — Draw exactly 1 Wavelength card each round.
- **Side choice** — Choose normal or weird side.
- **Target randomization** — Spin with screen closed.
- **Target check** — Open fully and inspect position.
- **Clue output** — Give one clue on the spectrum.
- **Post-clue lock** — Stop communicating immediately.

#### Psychic Team Actions
- **Discussion freedom** — Debate clue in any style.
- **Dial control** — Any teammate may turn dial.
- **Finalize position** — Announce locked dial to opponents.
- **Style guidance** — Avoid numbers/percentages for dial shorthand.

#### Opposing Team Actions
- **Binary decision** — Choose LEFT or RIGHT of final dial.
- **Marker placement** — Put guessing token into LEFT/RIGHT slot.

#### Edge-Case Actions
- **Center not visible** — Close screen and spin again.
- **Center partially visible at edge** — Valid target state.
- **Clue leak handling** — Table may reset round with new card/target by consensus.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Catch-Up Rule
- **Trigger** — Active team scores 4 points in a round.
- **Condition check** — That team is still losing after scoring.
- **Effect** — Same team immediately takes another turn.
- **Psychic rotation** — Use a different Psychic for that extra turn.

#### Clue Firm Rules
- **Single thought** — Do not combine multiple clue ideas.
- **No invention** — Clue must reference a thing that exists (real or established fictional).
- **On-topic only** — Clue must relate to current spectrum concepts.
- **No card-word family** — Do not use card words or direct synonyms.
- **No numeric coding** — No numbers, percentages, ratios, or numeric sneak hints.

#### Clue Suggestions
- **Concision** — Prefer short clues (often 5 words or fewer).
- **No soft modifiers** — Avoid “very,” “almost,” “kind of,” etc.
- **Concrete nouns** — Prefer specific names over vague “someone who…” phrasing.

#### Geometry FAQ Rule
- **Opposite-side sliver** — Ignore tiny visible wedge on opposite side.
- **True aim** — Aim relative to the side containing the center (4-point wedge).

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Round Scoring
- **Psychic team score** — Score wedge value where dial lands in target area (2, 3, or 4).
- **Edge tie on wedge line** — Psychic team gets the better of the two adjacent results.
- **Other team bonus** — Score 1 point if LEFT/RIGHT guess is correct.
- **Perfect-center exception** — If Psychic team hits 4-point center wedge, other team scores 0.

#### Game End Trigger
- **End threshold** — When a team reaches 10 points, game ends.
- **Winner rule** — Team with higher score wins.

#### Tie Procedure
1. **Sudden-death round** — Each team takes one final turn.
2. **Compare round points** — Include LEFT/RIGHT bonus in that round total.
3. **Repeat if tied** — Continue sudden-death rounds until one team wins.

#### All Scoring Sources
- **Source A** — Target wedge points (2-4) for active team.
- **Source B** — LEFT/RIGHT bonus point (1) for non-active team, except on perfect center.

#### Cooperative Mode Scoring (from excerpt)
--- 2-5 Players ---
- **Mode fit note** — Co-op mode is highlighted as working especially well.

--- Any Size ---
- **Mode legality** — Co-op mode is still allowed.

#### Cooperative Mode Rule Changes (from excerpt)
- **Deck setup** — Start with 7 random wavelength cards in slot.
- **End condition** — When that deck runs out, game ends.
- **Left/right phase** — Skip LEFT/RIGHT phase.
- **Center value** — Center wedge is worth 3 points in this mode.
- **Bonus round effect** — Hitting center grants a bonus card added to deck (adds an extra round).

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Opening Priorities
- **Calibrate endpoints** — First, anchor both ends of the spectrum with obvious examples.
- **Shared context first** — Psychics should use clues teammates will all recognize.
- **Dial protocol** — Decide who makes final dial adjustment before debate gets noisy.
- **Opponent read** — LEFT/RIGHT guesses should model Psychic-team intent, not your personal scale.
- **Psychic rotation** — Rotate clue givers often to avoid predictable clue style.

#### Player-Count Focus
--- 2-5 Players ---
- **Co-op recommendation** — Rulebook highlights this range as especially strong for cooperative mode.

--- Any Size ---
- **Co-op fallback** — Cooperative mode remains playable.

#### Fast Team Habit
- **10-second scan rule** — One player summarizes: “closer to left, near-mid, not extreme” before final dial lock.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Mistake 1 — Multi-part clue construction
- **Why it fails** — Team debates separate clue fragments and loses positional clarity.
- **Fix** — Give one atomic clue thought.

#### Mistake 2 — Psychic speaks after clue
- **Why it fails** — Clarification leaks hidden target direction.
- **Fix** — Enforce full silence after clue.

#### Mistake 3 — Numeric dial talk
- **Why it fails** — Numeric shorthand bypasses intended social inference.
- **Fix** — Use relative language (“more left,” “tiny right”).

#### Mistake 4 — Extreme anchoring bias
- **Why it fails** — Teams over-pull dial to endpoints and miss near-center targets.
- **Fix** — Place dial by relative distance between endpoints, not by dramatic examples.

#### Mistake 5 — Overthinking LEFT/RIGHT
- **Why it fails** — Binary phase becomes slow and less accurate.
- **Fix** — Make quick directional call from likely Psychic intent.

#### Mistake 6 — Rule-bending wordplay
- **Why it fails** — Off-topic puns or synonym loopholes distort fair clueing.
- **Fix** — Follow firm clue rules and keep clues on-spectrum.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1 — Precision clue vs robust clue
- **Precision line** — Higher 4-point upside, higher mismatch risk.
- **Robust line** — Safer team interpretation, often 2-3 point ceiling.

#### Decision 2 — Fast lock vs long debate
- **Fast lock** — Preserves first-instinct signal.
- **Long debate** — Can improve placement but increases anchoring drift.

#### Decision 3 — Center hunt vs guaranteed wedge
- **Center hunt** — Big swing and can deny opponent bonus if hit.
- **Guaranteed wedge** — Lower risk when team confidence is uncertain.

#### Decision 4 — Strict rules vs house flexibility
- **Strict mode** — Cleaner fairness and fewer disputes.
- **Flexible mode** — More casual vibe but higher ambiguity risk.

#### Decision 5 — Competitive vs cooperative mode
--- 2-5 Players ---
- **Co-op value** — Strong recommended range for shared-team experience.

--- Any Size ---
- **Co-op option** — Still legal if group prefers one-team play.

metadata.notes: Written from provided Wavelength rulebook excerpt as primary source. Component count for cards is not numerically visible in excerpt; used full-deck phrasing per provided text.
